source.title: Delirium: prevention, diagnosis and management in hospital and long-term care
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE legal and pathway adaptation
source.population: People aged 18 years and over in hospital or long-term residential or nursing care.
source.setting: Hospital and long-term care; the acute-hospital recommendations are partially applicable to emergency assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact only for recent fluctuating cognitive, perceptual, physical-function and social-behaviour changes, documentation of those changes, and use of a competent structured assessment. It does not establish delirium or cover every cause of altered mental status.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.3.1 — risk factors and indicators at presentation

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting recent hours-to-days changes or fluctuations in cognition, perception, physical function and social behaviour reported by the person or an informant.

items.rationale: The exact delirium section supports interval changes or fluctuations in cognition, perception, physical function and social behaviour reported by the person or informant without asserting persistent delirium.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting observed changes and, when performed by a competent practitioner, the named structured assessment and clinician-confirmed diagnosis; no diagnosis is inferred by the template.

source.title: Delirium: prevention, diagnosis and management in hospital and long-term care
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE legal and pathway adaptation
source.population: People aged 18 years and over in hospital or long-term residential or nursing care.
source.setting: Hospital and long-term care; the acute-hospital recommendations are partially applicable to emergency assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact only for recent fluctuating cognitive, perceptual, physical-function and social-behaviour changes, documentation of those changes, and use of a competent structured assessment. It does not establish delirium or cover every cause of altered mental status.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.5.2–1.6.4 — document changes and competent assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting observed changes and, when performed by a competent practitioner, the named structured assessment and clinician-confirmed diagnosis; no diagnosis is inferred by the template.

items.rationale: The exact section supports clinician-completed functional or cognitive assessment and caregiver information without auto-diagnosis.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting current prescribed, over-the-counter and complementary medicines, changes, discrepancies, discharge information, reconciliation and patient or carer involvement at care transitions.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation
source.population: Adults, children, and young people using medicines, with specific transfer, reconciliation, and decision-involvement recommendations.
source.setting: Transfers between care settings and clinician-led medicines consultations.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting current medicines, changes made during transfer, reconciliation, discrepancies, person or carer involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-led decisions. It does not generate a medicine start, stop, dose, prescription, or deprescribing decision.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.2–1.3.7 — transfer information and medicines reconciliation

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting current prescribed, over-the-counter and complementary medicines, changes, discrepancies, discharge information, reconciliation and patient or carer involvement at care transitions.

items.rationale: The exact transfer section supports reviewing discharge information, medicines and reconciliation after hospital discharge without changing a prescription.

items.final_wording: Supports flexible or prescheduled monitoring, review of emerging needs, a named coordinator, and documented care and support planning.

source.title: Dementia: assessment, management and support for people living with dementia and their carers
source.population: Adults with suspected or diagnosed dementia and their carers.
source.setting: Initial assessment, specialist assessment, ongoing care coordination, and support.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult memory and cognitive concern assessment and support; partial for undifferentiated memory symptoms and not directly validated for UAE referral, driving, or social-care pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.34–1.2.35 and 1.3.1–1.3.2 — monitoring, coordination, and support plans

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports flexible or prescheduled monitoring, review of emerging needs, a named coordinator, and documented care and support planning.

items.rationale: The exact support section supports documenting emerging needs, coordination, clinician-entered plans, follow-up and questions.
